text: Becky Biberstein Michael Green Frances Carr Tom Richhart Troy Cook Bobbie Jackson Kimberly Abney Sydnee Bradburn Lean ne Lee Jeff Tillman Mike Holland and Matt Norris Fourteenth Sunday in Ordinary Time July 5 2026 Welcome to all those joining us for the Fourth of July Holiday whether you are with us while visiting family or camping at the State Park May you have a blessed and relaxing holiday You are always welcome at St Joseph's This weekend July 4th 5th the Church celebrates the Fourteenth Sunday in Ordinary Time as our Nation celebrates the 250th Anniversary of the adoption of the Declaration of Independence by the 2nd Continental Congress Ask Father Thursday July 9th at 6:00PM Join us this Thursday July 9th at 6:00PM for Ask Father for an informal evening of questions and conversations Bring your questions about the Catholic Faith or simply come and listen We ll meet in the Narthex and choose a room in the Parish Center based on attendance Whether youve been Catholic for years or are just beginning to explore the faith all are welcome Masses at our Local Nursing Homes Throughout the month we offer Masses at several of our local and not so local retirement communities and care facilities In the past we had shared these Mass times along with our other weekday liturgies During COVID 19 however we stopped sharing these times to keep with requested restrictions regarding visitor policies at each facility We've recently revisited these policies with each facility and have received the go ahead to once again invite guests to join us and their residents for these Masses Of the five care facilities in our territory we currently offer Masses at least once a month at River Terrace Christian Care and Heritage Pointe in Warren where a community of our Victory Noll Sisters now live Mass is celebrated at River Terrace on the 2nd and 4th Thursdays of each month at 10:30AM in the sitting room off the main entryway Mass at Christian Care is celebrated on the 3rd Thursday of each month at 10:30AM in their Chapel Sunday Mass at Heritage Pointe in Warren is celebrated every Sunday at 1:00PM in their Chapel I celebrate this Mass on the 3rd Sunday of each month with other diocesan priests covering the other Sundays The above times are our normal Mass times but are subject to change depending on both my and the facilities schedules each month Please confirm these Mass times with our normal Parish Calendar if planning to attend Pontifical Mission Society Sr Mary Paul Asoegwu DDL Every year parishes in our diocese welcome missionary speakers assigned by our diocesan director for the Pontifical Mission Society This society supports the Church's missionary activity worldwide by fostering prayer promoting missionary awareness and providing material assistance to mission territories One way to promote this awareness is by welcoming our mission ary speakers as we learn about the missionary work that they participate in Next weekend we will welcome Sr Mary Paul Asoegwu of the Daughters of Divine Love Sr Asoegwu's community of religious sisters was founded on July 16 1969 in Nigeria partially in response to the brutal Nigeria Biafra War as a witness to Gods love throughout the world Today their community has over 1200 sisters most notably recognized by the signature blue veil worn as a part of their religious habit ministering in 15 countries around the world Sr Asoegwu will have the opportunity to speak briefly after our Masses next weekend and will be available to meet you in the narthex after each Mass In addition please see this weekend's insert that provides more information about the good work being done by the Daughters of Divine Love To offer financial support for their continued missionary work we will have a special Missions Collection Offertory Basket available at all Mass for donations Note For full fiscal transparency our diocese retains 10 of the annual mission collection to support our diocesan office of the Pontifical Mission Society The remaining 90 goes directly to the community being supported eg Daughters of Divine Love for their missionary work Saturday July 4 Independence Day
